introContext: Cold outbound. Larson Design Group is a 100% employee-owned (ESOP) multi-discipline AEC firm headquartered in Williamsport, PA — architecture, engineering, planning, surveying. ~700 employees across offices in the eastern US. Federal, transportation, energy, and municipal end-markets.
callObjective: Test whether LDG has a concrete bottleneck in their design / deliverable workflow (Revit interoperability, drawing-to-data extraction, automated quality checks, proposal-writing throughput) that a 2-week discovery sprint with Arvaya could quantify and propose to solve.
hypothesis: Multi-discipline AEC firms at LDG's scale (~700 staff) typically have deep Bentley/Autodesk stacks with custom glue between Revit, Civil 3D, MicroStation, and project-management tooling. The ML/AI org is usually thin or non-existent at this size — they buy or partner rather than build. The wedge for Arvaya is automation of the high-volume, low-creativity work: proposal drafting, deliverable QA, drawing-to-data extraction for as-built documentation.
meetingContext: First conversation — no prior contact. The AE will lead with discovery questions framed around their public ESOP narrative and federal-contract pipeline (they have a real fed/DOT/USACE practice). Outcome we want: identify one concrete workflow pain that's worth a follow-up scoping conversation. NOT trying to sell on this call.
competitiveContext: in-house build (their default for anything strategic)
Bentley iTwin / Autodesk Forge for platform-level integrations
Slalom or Accenture for generic AI consulting
